summary: We're a small healthcare office looking for a mid-level data scientist to help us make sense of patient data. Youll be working hands-on mostly with health records and some reporting, nothing fancy just practical stuff that helps us run better.

description.overview: At Cedar Valley Health Records you will use your data skills to analyze medical records, help us improve patient care by spotting trends, and keep the data organized and useful. You'll work with spreadsheets, databases, and a little bit of coding to turn numbers into answers that matter for a small operation. No big corporate stuff here, just straightforward daily data work to support healthcare workers behind the scenes.
responsibilities: Analyze patient data and spot trends that affect care quality
Build simple reports for doctors and staff to use during visits
Help maintain and clean the patient databases regularly
Work with Excel and SQL to handle health records data
Assist with small predictive models to forecast patient needs
Communicate findings in plain english to non-tech folks

required_skills: Data analysis in Python or R
SQL querying and basic database management
Experience cleaning and preparing datasets
Basic statistical knowledge
Reporting with Excel or similar tools
preferred_skills: Experience with healthcare or patient data
Basic knowledge of machine learning
Familiarity with HIPAA data rules
Visualization tools like Tableau or PowerBI
requirements.education: Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Statistics or related field or equal practical experiance

summary: We need a Senior Backend Engineer to help run the parts behind our fin-tech services. You'll fix bugs and build stuff to keep payments and data movin smooth. Just someone with good backend skills who can work hands-on not just talk tech.

description.overview: Sunny Side Payments is a small outfit that handles payment processing for local and regional merchants. We are lookin for a Senior Backend Engineer who can write and maintain code that runs our payment system backend. It means dealing with databases, APIs, keeping things secure, and fixing issues when they pop up. You'll mostly work on our existing platform, improve speed, and help make sure our customers get their payments on time without hassles. A no-nonsense, get-it-done approach is what we need. We dont expect fancy titles or big meetings, just solid code that works.
responsibilities: Write and maintain code for backend services handling transactions
Debug and fix bugs in the payment processing system
Work with database queries and optimize performance
Help keep data secure and compliant with basic fintech rules
Integrate third-party APIs for banking and payment gateways
Monitor system health and handle issues quickly
Collaborate occasionally with frontend or support folks to fix problems
Write simple documentation for the code you build or fix

required_skills: Backend developement with Python or Node.js
Experience with relational databases like Postgres or MySQL
Building and maintaining RESTful APIs
Basic security practices for financial data
Debugging and troubleshooting backend issues
preferred_skills: Knowledge of payment protocols and gateway integration
Familiarity with Docker or simple containerization
Experience with cloud services like AWS or Digital Ocean
requirements.education: Bachelor's degree in Computer Science or equivalent experience

summary: The incumbent will serve as a Data Scientist within a state public health agency, providing analytical support to guide evidence-based healthcare policies. This mid-level position requires adherence to established data governance protocols and collaborative engagement with cross-departmental teams.

description.overview: The Data Scientist will perform rigorous data analysis and modeling to support public health initiatives at the state level. Responsibilities include managing sensitive healthcare datasets in compliance with regulatory standards, conducting statistical analyses to inform policy decisions, and preparing comprehensive reports for internal and external stakeholders. The incumbent will operate under established procedural guidelines and contribute to ongoing efforts to improve the agency’s data infrastructure in a regulated, data-secure environment.
responsibilities: Develop and validate predictive models relevant to population health outcomes adhering to HIPAA and state data privacy laws.
Extract, clean, and manipulate large healthcare datasets ensuring compliance with agency data management policies.
Collaborate with epidemiologists and policy analysts to translate data findings into actionable public health recommendations.
Prepare technical documentation and reports consistent with state government formatting and review protocols.
Employ statistical software tools following prescribed methodologies to analyze trends and anomalies in healthcare data.
Participate in periodic audits and data quality assessments as mandated by public sector standards.

required_skills: Proficiency in R or Python for statistical analysis
Experience with SQL databases and data extraction
Knowledge of healthcare data standards and privacy regulations
Ability to produce formal data reports compliant with government guidelines
Applied experience in statistical modeling and data visualization
preferred_skills: Familiarity with public health informatics systems
Experience with ETL processes in government data environments
Understanding of state and federal health policy frameworks
requirements.education: Bachelor's degree in Statistics, Computer Science, Public Health, or a related quantitative discipline

summary: Join our dynamic, fast-paced environment as a Data Scientist rockstar to leverage cutting-edge synergy and drive best-in-class healthcare solutions. Collaborate cross-functionally to wear many hats and integrate diverse healthcare datasets for maximum synergy and impact.
Think outside the box to innovate and implement scalable data science workflows in a fast-paced environment.
Develop automated, best-in-class data pipelines that support real-time analytics and predictive modeling.
Spearhead exploratory data analysis and visualization initiatives to empower decision-makers with data-driven narratives.
Champion the adoption of cutting-edge AI techniques and thought leadership to maintain our world-class analytics edge.
Drive continuous improvement of data quality, governance, and operational efficiency to fuel business growth.

Experience with supervised and unsupervised machine learning algorithms
Strong background in statistical analysis and data visualization tools (e.g., Tableau, matplotlib, seaborn)
Familiarity with healthcare data standards such as HL7, FHIR, or related healthcare informatics

Knowledge of NLP techniques applicable to clinical and patient-generated text data
Familiarity with containerization tools such as Docker or Kubernetes

summary: At CartBlaze, we’re on a mission to make online shopping effortless and personal. As our Senior Product Manager, you’ll lead shaping and growing our core platform, owning product direction and pushing fast iterations that directly impact thousands of daily users.

In this role, you’ll steer cross-functional teams through quick pivots and learning cycles, balancing user needs with business impact. You’ll have real ownership over a product that’s growing fast, and the autonomy to wear multiple hats—from strategy to execution—inside a tight-knit startup culture committed to flexible work and meaningful impact.

Collaborate daily with engineers and designers to roll out features and fix pain points through rapid iteration cycles.
Analyze customer data and A/B test results to validate hypotheses and inform product decisions.
Work directly with marketing and sales to ensure product releases align with launch plans and user acquisition goals.
Manage backlog grooming and sprint planning to keep the team focused and efficient.
Champion the user's voice and translate it into practical, measurable product improvements.
Drive alignment across stakeholders through clear communication and progress updates.

Clear and persuasive communication skills across technical and non-technical teams
Ability to balance strategic thinking with hands-on execution
Proficiency in working with cross-functional teams including engineering, design, and marketing

Familiarity with user research methodologies
Knowledge of API-driven product integrations

summary: MedOptima Analytics seeks a mid-level Data Scientist to contribute to healthcare data analysis and predictive modeling initiatives. The candidate will apply statistical techniques and machine learning to improve patient outcomes within a structured corporate environment.

description.overview: The Data Scientist will operate within the Data Science department to analyze large-scale healthcare datasets, develop predictive models, and support data-driven decision-making processes. This role emphasizes accuracy, reproducibility, and compliance with healthcare data regulations, ensuring all models and insights align with corporate governance and clinical standards. Collaboration with cross-functional teams will be conducted through formal communication channels to deliver actionable insights for patient care improvement and operational efficiency.
responsibilities: Analyze healthcare data to identify trends and patterns relevant to clinical outcomes and operational performance.
Develop, validate, and deploy predictive models using machine learning techniques aligned with regulatory and corporate standards.
Collaborate with epidemiologists, biostatisticians, and IT to ensure data integrity and model reliability.
Prepare detailed technical reports and presentations adhering to corporate documentation policies.
Maintain compliance with HIPAA and internal security protocols during data handling and analysis.
Use statistical software and programming languages such as Python and R for data preparation and model development.
Monitor model performance post-deployment and iterate as necessary to sustain accuracy in clinical settings.

required_skills: Proficiency in Python and R for data analysis and modeling
Experience with healthcare datasets and HIPAA compliance
Strong understanding of machine learning algorithms and statistical methods
Experience with data visualization tools like Tableau or Power BI
Ability to document processes and results following strict corporate standards
preferred_skills: Familiarity with cloud platforms such as AWS or Azure for data science workloads
Knowledge of electronic health record (EHR) systems data structures
Experience in a regulated corporate environment within healthcare
requirements.education: Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Statistics, Data Science, or a related quantitative field

summary: Seeking a mid-level Data Scientist specializing in healthcare analytics to build scalable predictive models leveraging EHR, claims, and genomic data. The role demands expertise in HIPAA-compliant data pipelines and advanced statistical learning methods tailored to clinical outcomes and population health metrics.

description.overview: The Data Scientist will architect end-to-end machine learning workflows focused on risk stratification and patient cohort identification, utilizing large-scale, HIPAA-governed datasets exceeding tens of millions of records. You will operationalize models using Python and R packages (scikit-learn, XGBoost, TensorFlow) deployed on secure AWS EMR clusters, ensuring compliance with FHIR standards and HL7 v2 messaging. This role requires deep expertise in survival analysis, time-to-event modeling, and causal inference to produce actionable insights for precision medicine initiatives and value-based care optimization.
responsibilities: Design and implement ML models integrating EHR, claims, and genomic sequencing data under HIPAA constraints.
Develop ETL workflows in Spark/PySpark for datasets averaging 50TB in scale, optimizing throughput and latency within AWS EMR and S3 environments.
Apply survival and multistate models using lifelines or R's survival package for clinical risk prediction.
Collaborate with data engineers to enforce data governance aligning with HL7 FHIR and GDPR compliance.
Conduct statistical validation and calibration of models using bootstrapping, cross-validation, and AUC-ROC metrics.
Create and maintain dashboards with Tableau and Power BI to visualize patient stratification and cost reduction impact.
Implement NLP pipelines using medspaCy for clinical note processing and feature extraction.
Draft comprehensive model documentation adhering to FDA guidelines for software as a medical device (SaMD).

required_skills: Proficiency in Python (pandas, scikit-learn, XGBoost, TensorFlow) and R for statistical modeling
Experience with large-scale healthcare datasets formatted to FHIR and HL7 standards
Deep understanding of survival analysis, causal inference, and time-series modeling in clinical contexts
Hands-on knowledge of AWS EMR, S3, Spark, and secure data lake architectures
Expertise in HIPAA compliance and data privacy frameworks governing patient data
Familiarity with clinical NLP using medspaCy or similar tools
Strong statistical validation skills including bootstrapping and AUC analysis
preferred_skills: Experience with FDA software validation processes for SaMD
Knowledge of genomics data integration and variant calling pipelines
Use of Kubernetes or Docker for containerized model deployment
Experience building real-time predictive systems with Kafka or AWS Kinesis
requirements.education: Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Statistics, Biostatistics, Bioinformatics, or related quantitative field

summary: Quantiflow Technologies seeks a Senior Backend Engineer to architect and optimize scalable, low-latency microservices for real-time payments and risk analytics. The role demands deep expertise in high-throughput distributed systems, event-driven architecture, and compliance-ready data pipelines for financial services.

description.overview: As a Senior Backend Engineer at Quantiflow Technologies, you will lead the design and implementation of mission-critical backend systems powering real-time payment authorization, risk scoring, and regulatory reporting. Your focus will be on Kotlin/JVM-based microservices deployed on Kubernetes with CI/CD pipelines optimized for sub-50ms tail latency and over 10,000 TPS. You will work closely with data engineers building streaming ETL pipelines on Apache Kafka and ksqlDB, ensuring PCI DSS, SOX, and GDPR compliance. Your deep knowledge of ACID and BASE transaction models, REST/GRPC APIs with advanced OAuth2 scopes, and high-availability patterns will be essential to maintaining uptime SLAs exceeding 99.99%.
responsibilities: Design, build, and maintain Kotlin-based microservices for real-time payment processing and fraud detection handling >100M events/day.
Implement low-latency data ingestion and processing pipelines leveraging Apache Kafka, Confluent Schema Registry, and ksqlDB.
Optimize JVM performance including GC tuning, heap sizing, and thread concurrency to sustain SLA-driven sub-50ms request latency at scale.
Develop and enforce secure REST and gRPC APIs with OAuth2 authentication and granular RBAC for internal and external fintech partners.
Collaborate with DevOps to maintain automated CI/CD pipelines using Jenkins and ArgoCD ensuring zero-downtime deployments in EKS clusters.
Implement compliance and audit-ready data access controls compliant with PCI DSS, SOX, and GDPR standards within backend services.
Lead root cause analysis and resolution of production incidents impacting high-throughput payment workflows processing >500k TPS.
Provide technical mentorship and drive best practices around event-driven design, distributed transactions, and secure coding standards.

required_skills: Kotlin/JVM ecosystem including Spring Boot, Reactor, and Micrometer
Experience designing and scaling event-driven microservices using Apache Kafka and ksqlDB
Expertise in JVM performance tuning and garbage collection for high throughput low latency systems
In-depth knowledge of distributed transaction models, ACID vs BASE, and exactly-once processing semantics
Strong skills in designing secure REST and gRPC APIs with OAuth2, JWT, and RBAC
Familiarity with Kubernetes, Helm charts, and GitOps workflows in AWS EKS environments
Hands-on experience with CI/CD pipelines using Jenkins, ArgoCD or equivalent
Understanding of PCI DSS, SOX, GDPR compliance frameworks relevant to fintech platforms
preferred_skills: Experience with Scala or Java backend systems in financial services
Knowledge of Flink or Spark Streaming for complex event processing
Familiarity with Vault or Vaultless secret management solutions
Exposure to blockchain or distributed ledger technologies in payment contexts
requirements.education: Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or equivalent practical experience

Harness best-in-class tools to build scalable, maintainable, and high-performance software solutions.

summary: Seeking a mid-level Software Engineer to design, implement, and optimize scalable distributed systems with a focus on low-latency data pipelines and API performance. You will work within an agile, microservices-driven environment utilizing modern cloud infrastructure and container orchestration frameworks.

description.overview: As a Software Engineer at Infranetix Solutions, you will be responsible for developing and maintaining high-throughput distributed services primarily written in Golang and deployed on Kubernetes clusters across AWS and GCP. The role demands deep familiarity with designing RESTful and gRPC APIs, optimizing network IO under 10ms p99 latency constraints, and applying asynchronous processing via Kafka or NATS message brokers at 100K+ msgs/sec. You will collaborate closely with SRE teams to enforce observability best practices (Prometheus metrics, OpenTelemetry tracing) and implement robust CI/CD pipelines using Jenkins and Helm. Experience with binary serialization formats (Protobuf, Avro), relational (Postgres) and NoSQL (Cassandra) datastores, plus distributed consensus algorithms (Raft or Paxos) is vital for maintaining consistency and fault-tolerance at petabyte-scale workloads.
responsibilities: Develop and maintain scalable microservices in Go and Python targeting Kubernetes orchestration.
Design and optimize low-latency REST/gRPC APIs supporting up to 50,000 RPS with strict SLAs.
Implement distributed messaging workflows utilizing Kafka and NATS with at-least-once delivery guarantees.
Collaborate with DevOps to automate build, test, and deployment pipelines using Jenkins, Helm, and Terraform.
Integrate end-to-end telemetry using Prometheus, Grafana dashboards, and OpenTelemetry tracing.
Ensure data consistency across Postgres and Cassandra clusters using distributed locking and consensus protocols.
Perform root cause analysis of performance bottlenecks and latency spikes under high-concurrency scenarios.
Participate in code reviews and provide mentoring on clean, maintainable code aligned with SOLID principles.

required_skills: Proficient in Go and Python with experience in concurrent programming and network protocols
Strong knowledge of Kubernetes, Helm, and Docker containerization
Hands-on experience with Kafka or NATS streaming at scale (>100k messages/sec)
Deep understanding of REST/gRPC API design and performance tuning under 10ms latency targets
Practical experience with Prometheus metrics, OpenTelemetry distributed tracing, and Grafana
Familiarity with distributed databases (Postgres, Cassandra) and consensus algorithms (Raft, Paxos)
Using CI/CD tooling like Jenkins and infrastructure as code with Terraform
Experience balancing fault tolerance, availability, and consistency in large-scale distributed systems
preferred_skills: Exposure to cloud platforms AWS and GCP services (EKS, Cloud SQL, Pub/Sub)
Knowledge of binary serialization formats such as Protobuf and Avro
Experience with service mesh technologies like Istio or Linkerd
Background in site reliability engineering or performance engineering
requirements.education: Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Computer Engineering, or equivalent practical experience
